issue.title: Add named helper metadata for generated WP_Http::request adapter

artifacts.role: metadata parser accepting primary and named @:wp.haxeHelper adapter helpers
WordPress adapter registry consuming named helpers for WP_Http::request safety and stream decisions
annotated request shell proving primary plus named helper metadata
behavior manifest requiring named helper IR features and generated shell calls
public-shell snapshot manifest proving byte-stable generated request shell shape
policy manifest preserving zero raw-block and zero template counts

claims: WPHX PHP adapter metadata now supports a primary helper plus named helper aliases on one public method.
The generated WP_Http::request original-path shell can call typed Haxe helpers for safety-option and stream-blocking branch decisions without runner string patches.
The existing WPHX-312.65 nonblocking behavior gate remains green while public-shell snapshots cover the named helper calls.
non_claims: This does not claim full WP_Http::request ownership.
This does not retire every existing copied WP_Http::request candidate shell yet.
This does not make WPHX PHP a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
